fake_suspect.name: Geoffrey Hartley
fake_suspect.framing_reason: Edmund's estranged brother who stood to inherit the family estate and had publicly threatened Edmund over a business dispute two weeks prior

planted_evidence.description: A receipt for arsenic trioxide purchased from a chemical supply company found in Geoffrey's hotel room wastebasket
Handwritten notes detailing the gallery's layout and Edmund's typical champagne preferences discovered in Geoffrey's rental car
Text messages on a burner phone showing Geoffrey coordinating with an unknown accomplice about 'the gallery event'

false_testimonies.claim: I saw Geoffrey Hartley lurking near the champagne service area around 18:55, acting suspiciously and watching Edmund intently
Geoffrey approached me earlier in the evening asking which glass Edmund would be drinking from, claiming he wanted to make a surprise toast to reconcile
Camera 7 malfunctioned naturally due to age, but I reviewed footage before it went down and saw Geoffrey entering the service corridor near the champagne preparation area
Geoffrey had sent me threatening letters about Edmund in the past month, warning that Edmund would 'pay for his betrayals' at a public event

fake_timeline.event: Geoffrey Hartley arrives at gallery but remains outside, watching through windows
Geoffrey enters through service entrance and accesses champagne preparation area undetected
Geoffrey poisons a specific champagne glass intended for Edmund, then exits quickly
Geoffrey watches from the crowd as Edmund receives the poisoned glass during the toast
Geoffrey slips out during the confusion as Edmund collapses
Geoffrey returns to his hotel, disposing of poison container in public trash bin two blocks away

evidence_fabrications.E001: The champagne glass fragments contain arsenic residue that matches the chemical signature of the supply company receipt found in Geoffrey's possession
evidence_fabrications.E002: Security camera 7 footage gap was caused by Geoffrey's accomplice creating a technical malfunction to hide his access to the service area
evidence_fabrications.E003: The shipping manifest fragment found in the furnace was actually from Geoffrey's poison purchase, carelessly discarded by him during his escape
evidence_fabrications.E004: Vivienne's ring tested positive for arsenic because Geoffrey brushed against her during the crowd confusion, transferring trace amounts
evidence_fabrications.E005: The waiter's testimony about Charlotte's champagne instructions was actually about Geoffrey disguised as service staff giving those same instructions
evidence_fabrications.E006: Dr. Pemberton's medical bag contained standard emergency supplies; any arsenic traces came from treating Edmund and environmental contamination
evidence_fabrications.E007: The false alarm in the sculpture room was triggered accidentally by gallery equipment malfunction, coincidentally benefiting Geoffrey's escape timing
evidence_fabrications.E008: Thomas Blackwood's maintenance logs show the camera failure was spontaneous, but Geoffrey had researched the gallery's aging security system beforehand

conspirator_alibis.Dr. Marcus Pemberton: I was mingling with guests in the sculpture wing from 18:30 to 19:20, discussing art acquisitions with several collectors who can verify my presence, then responded immediately to Edmund's collapse as any physician would
conspirator_alibis.Charlotte Devereaux: I was coordinating with catering staff throughout the evening as the event manager, ensuring proper service flow, and was in the kitchen area from 18:55 to 19:15 reviewing the dessert presentation schedule with the head chef
conspirator_alibis.Thomas Blackwood: I was conducting routine security rounds of the perimeter and upper galleries from 19:00 to 19:20, as documented in my security log, and only learned of camera 7's malfunction when I returned to the control room
conspirator_alibis.Vivienne Ashford: I was hosting guests and fulfilling my duties as gallery owner, speaking with Edmund briefly about a potential acquisition, then making the traditional ceremonial toast as I do at every opening event
